topic: Preference-Based Learning in Audio Applications: A Systematic Analysis
topic_definition: Operational title/abstract decision table. Include only when ALL of the following are satisfied: (A) audio-domain evidence is present, (B) preference-signal evidence is present, and (C) the preference/ranking signal has a learning role (training objective, loss, reward modeling, label construction, or model selection). Audio-domain strong positives include speech, spoken dialogue/calls, speech emotion recognition, acoustic/audio signal, voice/paralinguistic cues, or canonical speech corpora context (for example IEMOCAP/SEMAINE). For multi-modal work, audio must be a core input modality, not peripheral metadata. Preference-signal positives include pairwise comparison, relative preference labels, rank/rank-order supervision, preference optimization, or human comparative judgments used for learning. Ordinal/rank formulations count only when they are used to learn ordered preferences (not merely reported labels). Insufficient evidence includes emotion/ranking wording without clear audio context, scalar rating-only prediction, pure category classification labels, or ranking used only in evaluation/reporting. Preference-only-for-evaluation papers are excluded.

summary_topics.description: Preference-Based Learning in Audio Applications: A Systematic Analysis

required.criterion: Learning-role gate (required): preference/ranking signal must explicitly affect learning (training objective/loss/reward, label construction, or model selection). Signals used only for evaluation/benchmark reporting do not satisfy inclusion.

Preference-signal gate (required): at least one positive preference-like signal is present, such as pairwise comparison, relative labels, rank/rank-order learning, preference optimization, or comparative human judgments converted to training supervision.

Audio-domain gate (required): title/abstract must indicate audio or speech as core task signal. Strong positives include speech/SER, spoken interactions or calls, acoustic/audio/voice/paralinguistic framing, or canonical speech corpora context. Multi-modal papers are in-scope only when audio is core.
Paper is not a survey/review article.

exclusion_criteria.criterion: Negative override: preference or ranking appears only in evaluation/reporting/annotation description and is not used for learning/optimization/training.
Negative override: pure SER or emotion classification/regression without a preference/rank-based learning component.
Negative override: ordinal intensity labels are present but not operationalized as preference/rank learning signals in the model objective.
Negative override: not an audio-domain work, or multimodal with no clear evidence that audio is core.
